channel.title: Young Bull Desk Notes (Members)
channel.description: The note the desk files every trading day. Members only; this feed URL is your key, do not share it.

item.title: Desk Note, 2026-07-23
item.description: MU led the book again today, up 3.2% to $990.21 and sitting about 117% above my $456.89 cost basis, the same day the scout's Intel Q2 data-center print line quietly resolved in the direction the memory thesis has been pointing all along. CIFR was the sharpest mover in the sleeve, up 5.56%, and CRDO added another 3.61%, while ASTS gave back 4.47% and ALMU and ARM traded close to flat. NBIS held a steady gain, up 1.29% and about 124% above cost. None of today's moves are anywhere near a thesis-break level for any anchor, it's just the physical-layer stuff compounding quietly while the noisier names bounce around it.

item.title: Desk Note, 2026-07-22
item.description: MU pulled back today, down 1.17% to $959.48, still up about 110% from my $456.89 cost basis after yesterday's DRAM-shortage pop. NBIS barely moved, up 0.57% and sitting near 121% above cost, while CIFR was the real mover in the book, up 6.86%. Most of the rest of the compute and photonics sleeve traded red and small, ARM down 2.18%, ASTS down 2.19%, ALMU down 2.5%, nothing close to a thesis break. Today's scout scan came back thin, the same HBM and cooling-layer stories from SemiAnalysis and Temple8 that have circulated for a week and a half straight, which tells me the market hasn't priced in anything new since yesterday's memory story broke. Slow days like this are where the discipline rules matter more than the green days do.

item.title: Desk Note, 2026-07-21
item.description: MU is at $970.82 today, up 112.5% from a $456.89 cost basis, running another 12.17% in a single session the same day r/hardware puts a 9-out-of-10 score on a thread calling out Micron, SK Hynix, and Samsung for DRAM price hikes as high as 600%. It wasn't the only green name in the book, NBIS ran 18.78% to $216.92 and now sits almost 120% above cost, OUST added 17%, ALMU added 12.39%, CIFR added 11.44%, close to every position I hold moved together today. That's not one name having a good day, that's the whole memory and compute layer getting repriced the same day the scarcity complaint went viral, and a scarcity complaint from the people paying the higher price is usually the best confirmation a thesis is working. No trim call off any of this yet, just the tape doing what the thesis said it would do.

item.title: Desk Note, 2026-07-20
item.description: MU is sitting at $865.46 today, up about 89.4% from cost, and the scout desk still can't clear a single headline that isn't the same HBM bottleneck story this position was bought for, SemiAnalysis's Huawei Ascend ramp piece and its Scaling the Memory Wall roadmap both back at the top of today's scan. NBIS is right there with it at $182.62, up roughly 85.2% from cost, the other anchor still compounding on the exact same buildout. XNDU had the loudest tape in the book today, up 8.51% and now sitting about 69.4% above cost, while CIFR ran even harder intraday, up 16.97%, though it's barely above cost lifetime at 1.2%. PENG gave back 11.89% today but still holds an 11.2% lifetime gain, and OUST slipped 6.13% today while staying up about 2.1% from cost. Temple8's Western data center cooling play is still on the scout too, the one physical layer slot on the map still waiting on anchor money. Nothing in the book needs a trim call off any of this yet.

item.title: Desk Note, 2026-07-19
item.description: MU is holding at $848.95 today, up about 85.8% from cost, while the scout desk is still stacked with the same HBM bottleneck story this position was bought for, SemiAnalysis's Huawei Ascend ramp piece and its "Scaling the Memory Wall" roadmap both sitting at the top again. NBIS sits right beside it at $177.71, up roughly 80.2% from cost, the other anchor compounding on the same buildout. SILC had the loudest tape in the book today, up 9.96%, with OUST up 5.41% and ASTS up 5.07% even though ASTS is still about 3.3% underwater from cost overall. PENG gave back 8.43% today but is still sitting on a 26.2% lifetime gain, and ARM ticked up 1.98% while sitting almost dead flat against its own cost basis, still waiting on its catalyst. Temple8's Western data center cooling play is still on the scout too, the one Physical Layer slot on the map with nothing anchored in it yet. LPKFF and LUMN stay the reminder that not every name in the book gets the toll booth treatment, down 38.1% and 37.3% from cost while the anchors keep compounding right beside them.

item.title: Desk Note, 2026-07-18
item.description: MU is up 85.8% from cost and NBIS is sitting right next to it at 80.2%, the two anchors still doing exactly what the toll booth thesis said they would. SILC ripped 9.96% today and OUST and ASTS both had strong green sessions, up 5.41% and 5.07%, even with ASTS still down about 3.3% from cost overall. PENG gave back 8.43% on the day but is still up 26.2% lifetime, and ARM popped 1.98% today but is still just barely underwater from cost, down about 0.03%, still waiting on its own catalyst. the HBM bottleneck story is back on the scout again, this time with r/hardware flagging AMD's own HBM-loaded MI350P showing up inside real enterprise servers right behind Huawei's forced Ascend ramp, another buyer added to the same finite memory supply. LPKFF and LUMN are the reminder that not every name in the book gets the toll booth treatment, down 38.1% and 37.3% from cost while the anchors keep compounding right beside them.

item.title: Desk Note, 2026-07-17
item.description: SILC ripped +9.96% today, PENG dropped -8.43%, and MU just sits there up 86% from cost while nobody's watching it. NBIS had a good one too, up 3.46% on the day and now +80% lifetime, the same week SemiAnalysis is back on the HBM bottleneck story (Huawei's forced Ascend ramp, the "Scaling the Memory Wall" piece) for the third day running. none of that HBM noise moves ARM though, basically flat at -0.03% from cost, still waiting on its own catalyst. the real headline today isn't any single ticker, it's r/energy's read that Trump's spent billions on energy and the electricity boom still hasn't shown up, a reminder that power gets built by whoever actually ships gigawatts, not by who wins the subsidy fight. OUST and ASTS both had strong green days, +5.41% and +5.07%, even with ASTS still sitting about 3% underwater from cost. that's still the layer worth watching closest.

item.title: Desk Note, 2026-07-16
item.description: every name in my book is red today, and I'm still sitting on a +87% gain in my biggest anchor. ASTS led the drop at -17%, NBIS -14%, IQMX -13%, RKLB -12%, and CIFR -11%, a broad risk-off day across the whole Physical Layer sleeve, not just one ticker. it landed the same day New York signed the first statewide freeze on new AI hyperscale data centers over rising power costs. none of that changes the anchor math: MU still +87% from cost, NBIS +74%, ALMU +17%, ARM basically flat at -2%. red days test the discipline rules, not the thesis, and the rules haven't fired on anything today.

item.title: Desk Note, 2026-07-15
item.description: The book took a real hit today: MU dropped just over 8% and Ouster gave back nearly 10%, with most of the Physical Layer red across the board. The wire is still hammering the same chokepoint story, SemiAnalysis ran fresh pieces this week on Huawei's forced Ascend ramp and on what they call the memory wall, and both land on HBM supply as the actual constraint, not chip design. That is the exact thesis MU was bought for, so a down day on the ticker does not touch the thesis. I am still up about 98% on my MU cost basis, the anchor stays intact. NBIS bucked the tape at plus 2.8% and has now more than doubled off my entry, a reminder the buildout does not trade as one block.
